audit_items.proof_trace: The only non-stack pointer in entropy.c is the `buf` parameter; there is no null guard at function entry, but every call site guarantees a non-NULL argument: passwd.c L320-323 performs an explicit null check after malloc before calling at L324, and all other callers (passwd.c L766, L818, L877; uuid.c L173, L349) pass stack-allocated arrays or stack variable addresses which are never NULL by C semantics. The `gettimeofday(..., NULL)` at L144 is intentional (NULL timezone pointer is safe per POSIX). The defensive gap is real but no externally-reachable LDAP path can deliver a NULL buf to the function.
